title: Status, Events, Logs, Metrics, Traces, Audit, and Layered Incident Response · Platform Workshop
summary: A platform pairing session predicts each API transition before producing the first working cluster and workload incident evidence record for status, events, logs, metrics, traces, audit, and layered incident response. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of cluster and workload incident evidence record while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Correlate spec, observedGeneration, conditions, reasons, messages, events, resource versions, controller ownership, and event retention without treating event order as durable history.
Collect current and previous container logs, multi-container streams, timestamps, kubelet and runtime logs, rotation, retention, and node-loss boundaries.
Distinguish resource metrics, component metrics, application metrics, state metrics, cardinality, scrape, storage, alert, and autoscaling evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop K8s Status Conditions Events
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop workload restarts after revision 4; logs rotated, metrics aggregate away one replica, events are delayed, and an operator deletes the Pod first. Preserve object status, events, current and previous logs, metrics, traces, node evidence, and a causal timeline. Competency probe: Correlate spec, observedGeneration, conditions, reasons, messages, events, resource versions, controller ownership, and event retention without treating event order as durable history.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop K8s Status Conditions Events.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for correlate spec, observedgeneration, conditions, reasons, messages, events, resource versions, controller ownership, and event retention without treating event order as durable history. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop K8s Status Conditions Events, keep K8s Status Conditions Events connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A workshop workload restarts after revision 4; logs rotated, metrics aggregate away one replica, events are delayed, and an operator deletes the Pod first. Preserve object status, events, current and previous logs, metrics, traces, node evidence, and a causal timeline. Competency probe: Correlate spec, observedGeneration, conditions, reasons, messages, events, resource versions, controller ownership, and event retention without treating event order as durable history.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which K8s Status Conditions Events condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Kubernetes Observability and Debugging Documentation — Controls status, conditions, events, application and system logs, metrics, traces, audit correlation, retention, cardinality, and layered incident response.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop K8s Layered Debugging Method
steps.instruction:
Append the Kubernetes evidence item headed "  # Evidence: k8s-layered-debugging-method-tmls4p". Tailor its incident-evidence.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic API or manifest review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run kubectl, Helm, kubeadm, and cluster commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop K8s Layered Debugging Method.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns K8s Layered Debugging Method into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop K8s Layered Debugging Method, keep K8s Layered Debugging Method connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-layered-debugging-method-tmls4p
  - competency: k8s-layered-debugging-method
    artifact: incident-evidence.yaml
    decision: layered debugging method
    environment: clean conformance cluster with only required add-ons installed
    fault-injection: make an admission dependency unavailable during an upgrade
    retained-proof: EndpointSlice DNS policy route and packet-path correlation
    case-key: tmls4p
    change: |
      correlation: release-uid-request-trace
      status: generation-and-conditions
      events: timestamped-short-retention
      logs: current-previous-and-node
      metrics: resource-system-application
      audit: identity-verb-resource
      diagnosis: first-causal-layer
    verify: kubectl get deployment,pod,endpointslice -o json && kubectl logs status-api --previous --timestamps
    failure: Reject green replica count as user proof, restart as diagnosis, events as durable history, kubectl logs as archival storage, or unbounded cardinality.
    repair: Preserve identity and time, correlate desired and observed layers, reproduce one fault cleanly, retain the regression, and document telemetry limits.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.
